cases.passage: Raj was a quiet and reserved person. He rarely spoke in class, and whenever he did, his words were laced with depth and meaning. His classmates admired him for his unique perspective on life. Mrs. Mathur, their English teacher, was particularly fond of Raj. She encouraged him to participate more in class, but Raj preferred to listen and observe. One day, while working on a group project, Raj's group members were at a loss for ideas. Raj's silence was starting to get on their nerves, but just when they thought they had exhausted all possibilities, Raj spoke up with a brilliant solution. His group members were amazed and grateful for his contribution.

questions.q: What impression does Raj create on his classmates?
opts: (A) He is an outgoing and confident person.
(B) He is a quiet and reserved person.
(C) He is an average student.
(D) He is a poor listener.
questions.explanation: Raj is described as a quiet and reserved person who rarely speaks in class.

questions.q: How does Mrs. Mathur encourage Raj to participate in class?
opts: (A) She scolds him for not speaking up.
(B) She ignores him in class.
(C) She encourages him to participate more.
(D) She gives him extra homework.
questions.explanation: Mrs. Mathur is fond of Raj and encourages him to participate more in class.

questions.q: What does Raj's group members feel about his silence?
opts: (A) They admire his silence.
(B) They are annoyed by his silence.
(C) They are indifferent to his silence.
(D) They are surprised by his silence.
questions.explanation: Raj's group members are at a loss for ideas and his silence is starting to get on their nerves.

questions.q: What does Raj contribute to his group project?
opts: (A) A brilliant solution.
(B) A poor idea.
(C) A mediocre suggestion.
(D) No contribution at all.
questions.explanation: Raj speaks up with a brilliant solution that amazes his group members.

questions.q: What is Raj's preferred way of interacting with his classmates?
opts: (A) He likes to be the center of attention.
(B) He prefers to listen and observe.
(C) He likes to speak up in class.
(D) He likes to give lengthy presentations.
questions.explanation: Raj prefers to listen and observe rather than speaking up in class.

cases.passage: Lena's family had always been supportive of her dreams, but her father's recent illness had made it difficult for her to continue her studies. Despite her best efforts, she found herself struggling to keep up with her coursework. One day, while working on a project, Lena's friend, Ralph, noticed her frustration. He offered to help her with the project, but Lena was hesitant at first. She didn't want to burden him with her problems. However, Ralph's kind words and gentle encouragement eventually won her over, and she accepted his offer. Together, they worked on the project, and Lena was amazed at how easily the tasks fell into place with Ralph's help.

questions.q: Why was Lena struggling to continue her studies?
opts: (A) She had lost interest in her coursework.
(B) She had too many extracurricular activities.
(C) Her father's recent illness had made it difficult for her to focus.
(D) She was facing financial difficulties.
questions.explanation: Lena's father's recent illness had made it difficult for her to continue her studies.

questions.q: Why was Lena hesitant to accept Ralph's offer of help?
opts: (A) She didn't want to burden him with her problems.
(B) She was afraid of failing again.
(C) She thought she could handle it on her own.
(D) She didn't trust him.
questions.explanation: Lena didn't want to burden Ralph with her problems.

questions.q: What did Ralph offer to help Lena with?
opts: (A) Her coursework.
(B) Her project.
(C) Her extracurricular activities.
(D) Her personal problems.
questions.explanation: Ralph offered to help Lena with her project.

questions.q: How did Lena feel after accepting Ralph's offer of help?
opts: (A) She felt guilty for burdening him.
(B) She felt relieved and happy.
(C) She felt frustrated and annoyed.
(D) She felt indifferent.
questions.explanation: Lena was amazed at how easily the tasks fell into place with Ralph's help.

questions.q: What did Ralph's kind words and gentle encouragement do to Lena?
opts: (A) They made her more frustrated.
(B) They made her more determined to fail.
(C) They eventually won her over and made her accept his offer of help.
(D) They didn't have any effect on her.
questions.explanation: Ralph's kind words and gentle encouragement eventually won Lena over and made her accept his offer of help.
